helpful: Provisioned questions-presented.txt / petition.txt / brief-in-opposition.txt — posture and vehicle defects were decisive context
statpack relist-count cut — quantified how far this ~15-relist docket sits outside the normal cert population
Web retrieval in forward mode — the July 2, 2026 companion-case grants (Grant v. Higgins / Viramontes) completely reframed the prediction
gaps: fedcourts query citation matching returned nothing for Heller/Bruen citers; a keyword or caption search over resolved priors would have surfaced Snope/Harrel/Ocean State Tactical analogs
No statpack cut for held-companion petitions (disposition split of petitions pending while a same-QP case is on the merits docket) — that is the dominant base rate for this cell
supremecourt.gov docket fetch is blocked (403); a provisioned post-order-list docket refresh or an MCP route to the SCOTUS docket would remove the need to infer current status from secondary sources
notes: Two corpus query calls transferred ~109 MB each via ranged reads and matched nothing; for citation-based retrieval that cost/benefit is poor. The committed statpack covered the base-rate need.
